steps.title: Identify the target meaning
steps.content: The passage states that artworks have become inaccessible because they relied on software "(most notably Adobe Flash, discontinued in 2021)." The blank describes software that no longer operates — it was shut down, not just obscure, unnecessary, or widespread.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘defunct’: Means no longer operating or existing — fits Adobe Flash exactly; it was discontinued and can no longer run the artworks. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘extraneous’: Means unnecessary or irrelevant — contradicts the passage, which says the artworks require the software; it is not superfluous. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘arcane’: Means obscure or known only to a few — describes something hard to understand, not something unavailable; Flash was once mainstream. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘ubiquitous’: Means found everywhere — the opposite of the problem; if the software were widely available, the artworks would not be inaccessible. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
